yield.aprApy.closing: Always compare APR to APR and APY to APY. Comparing one protocol's APR to another's APY makes the second look worse than it actually is.
yield.aprApy.field1.label: Base Rate
yield.aprApy.field2.label: APR (no compounding)
yield.aprApy.field3.label: Compounding Frequency
yield.aprApy.field4.label: APY
yield.aprApy.p1: Annual Percentage Rate (APR) is a simple yearly rate with no compounding: it assumes you never reinvest what you earn. Annual Percentage Yield (APY) assumes earnings are reinvested continuously, so it compounds over the course of the year.
yield.aprApy.p2: The same underlying rate produces a higher APY than APR, purely because of compounding, which is why protocols often advertise the larger APY number even when the actual rate paid is more modest.
yield.aprApy.title: APR and APY
yield.compounding.auto.text: A protocol reinvests rewards for you continuously, usually in exchange for a small performance fee.
yield.compounding.auto.title: Automatic Compounding
yield.compounding.closing: Frequent manual compounding can be eaten up by transaction fees on some networks, which is part of why automatic compounding vaults exist.
yield.compounding.manual.text: You reinvest rewards yourself, paying a transaction fee each time you do.
yield.compounding.manual.title: Manual Compounding
yield.compounding.p1: Compounding means reinvesting the yield you've already earned so that future returns are calculated on a larger base, rather than withdrawing rewards as they arrive.
yield.compounding.p2: The more frequently rewards compound, the higher the resulting APY, which is exactly the relationship covered earlier: identical base rates produce different APYs depending on how often they compound.
yield.compounding.title: Compounding
yield.emissions.closing: An APY that's mostly emissions is really a temporary subsidy, not a return generated by the protocol's actual activity.
yield.emissions.li1: Bootstrapping liquidity. Emissions can attract deposits quickly when a protocol is new and has little organic activity yet.
yield.emissions.li2: Diluting existing holders. Every new token distributed slightly reduces the share everyone else's tokens represent.
yield.emissions.li3: Fading over time. Most emissions schedules are designed to decrease, meaning the advertised APY funded by them typically falls as the protocol matures.
yield.emissions.p1: Emissions are new tokens a protocol creates and distributes as rewards, usually to attract early liquidity before trading fees or interest alone would be enough to compete with other protocols.
yield.emissions.p2: Because emissions are newly created supply, distributing them increases the total number of tokens in circulation, which puts downward pressure on that token's price unless demand grows just as fast.
yield.emissions.title: Token Emissions
yield.glossary.def1: Any return earned on deposited assets, from staking rewards, trading fees, interest, or token emissions.
yield.glossary.def10: A plan for where to deposit assets, and how (or whether) to move them, to capture return.
yield.glossary.def2: Annual Percentage Rate: a yearly return with no compounding assumed.
yield.glossary.def3: Annual Percentage Yield: a yearly return that assumes earnings are reinvested and compound over time.
yield.glossary.def4: Total Value Locked: the total value of assets currently deposited in a protocol.
yield.glossary.def5: New tokens a protocol creates and distributes as rewards, increasing that token's circulating supply.
yield.glossary.def6: Return paid from a protocol's actual fees or interest, rather than from newly minted tokens.
yield.glossary.def7: Reinvesting earned yield so future returns are calculated on a larger base.
yield.glossary.def8: Actively moving assets between protocols to capture the highest available return.
yield.glossary.def9: The reduction in each token holder's relative share caused by new token emissions.
yield.glossary.title: Glossary
yield.hero.description: Not all yield is created equal. How to separate staking rewards, trading fees and token emissions, and tell a sustainable return from a temporary incentive.
yield.hero.stat1.label: Difficulty
yield.hero.stat2.label: Duration
yield.hero.stat3.label: Format
yield.hero.stat4.label: Sections
yield.hero.title: Yield
yield.introduction.p1: Modules 05 and 06 covered two places yield can come from: trading fees earned by liquidity providers, and interest paid by borrowers. This lesson steps back and asks a broader question: when a protocol advertises a return, where does that number actually come from, and will it still be there next month.
yield.introduction.p2: This lesson explains the real sources yield can come from, how APR and APY differ, why Total Value Locked (TVL) is a useful signal but not a guarantee, and how to recognize when an advertised yield exists only to attract deposits rather than to last.
yield.introduction.p3: By the end of this lesson you will be able to look past a headline percentage and ask the one question that matters: is this yield being paid out of real activity, or is it being paid out of new deposits.
yield.introduction.title: Introduction
yield.meta.title: Yield Fundamentals - Nodra Academy
yield.quiz.intro: Check your understanding of this lesson before moving on. Incorrect answers point you straight back to the relevant section.
yield.quiz.q1: 1. Which of these is one of the three main sources of yield covered in this lesson?
yield.quiz.q1.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Sources of Yield and try again.
yield.quiz.q1o1: A. Token emissions distributed to attract deposits
yield.quiz.q1o3: C. A fixed fee charged only to new users
yield.quiz.q2: 2. Why is APY typically higher than APR for the same base rate?
yield.quiz.q2.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review APR and APY and try again.
yield.quiz.q2o1: A. APY includes a bonus paid only to early depositors
yield.quiz.q2o2: B. APY accounts for compounding, reinvesting earnings over the year
yield.quiz.q2o3: C. APY is calculated using a different currency
yield.quiz.q2o4: D. APY excludes protocol fees, while APR includes them
yield.quiz.q3: 3. What does a high TVL actually suggest about a protocol?
yield.quiz.q3.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Total Value Locked and try again.
yield.quiz.q3o1: A. That deposited funds are guaranteed to be safe
yield.quiz.q3o2: B. That a meaningful amount of real capital is currently deployed in it
yield.quiz.q3o3: C. That the protocol's token price will only go up
yield.quiz.q3o4: D. That the protocol has passed a government audit
yield.quiz.q4: 4. What effect do token emissions have on a protocol's existing token holders?
yield.quiz.q4.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Token Emissions and try again.
yield.quiz.q4o1: A. They increase each holder's share of the total supply
yield.quiz.q4o2: B. They dilute existing holders by increasing the total supply
yield.quiz.q4o3: C. They have no effect on circulating supply
yield.quiz.q4o4: D. They automatically raise the token's price
yield.quiz.q5.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Real Yield vs Emissions and try again.
yield.quiz.q5o1: A. Real yield is always higher than emissions yield
yield.quiz.q5o2: B. Real yield is paid from actual fees or interest, not newly minted tokens
yield.quiz.q5o3: C. Emissions yield is paid only to a protocol's founders
yield.quiz.q5o4: D. Real yield can only be earned through staking
yield.quiz.q6: 6. Which best describes "active farming" as a yield strategy?
yield.quiz.q6.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Yield Strategies and try again.
yield.quiz.q6o1: A. Depositing once and never checking the position again
yield.quiz.q6o2: B. Regularly moving capital between protocols to chase the highest current incentive
yield.quiz.q6o3: C. A strategy that eliminates impermanent loss entirely
yield.quiz.q6o4: D. A strategy only available to institutional investors
yield.quiz.q7: 7. How does compounding frequency affect APY?
yield.quiz.q7.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Compounding and try again.
yield.quiz.q7o1: A. More frequent compounding always lowers the resulting APY
yield.quiz.q7o2: B. More frequent compounding produces a higher resulting APY for the same base rate
yield.quiz.q7o3: C. Compounding frequency has no effect on APY
yield.quiz.q7o4: D. Compounding only applies to token emissions, not fees
yield.quiz.q8: 8. What is a warning sign that an advertised yield may be unsustainable?
yield.quiz.q8.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Evaluating Sustainability and try again.
yield.quiz.q8o1: A. The yield is paid partly through trading fees
yield.quiz.q8o2: B. The yield would disappear without a constant stream of new deposits funding it
yield.quiz.q8o3: C. The protocol displays both APR and APY
yield.quiz.q8o4: D. TVL has remained stable over several months
yield.quiz.q9: 9. What is the single most useful question to ask before trusting an advertised yield number?
yield.quiz.q9.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite, review Evaluating Sustainability and try again.
yield.quiz.q9o1: A. Whether the protocol has a popular social media account
yield.quiz.q9o2: B. Where the yield actually comes from
yield.quiz.q9o3: C. How many other protocols offer a similar number
yield.quiz.q9o4: D. Whether the yield is paid daily or weekly
yield.quiz.title: Quiz
yield.realVsEmissions.closing: A protocol advertising 40% APY made almost entirely of emissions is telling you something very different than one advertising 4% APY made entirely of trading fees.
yield.realVsEmissions.emissions.text: Paid in newly created tokens, only valuable if that token holds or grows its price.
yield.realVsEmissions.emissions.title: Emissions Yield
yield.realVsEmissions.p1: "Real yield" describes returns paid from a protocol's actual revenue, trading fees or borrowing interest, in an asset that already has independent value, rather than in newly minted tokens.
yield.realVsEmissions.p2: Emissions yield can still be worth participating in, but it should be understood for what it is: a reward paid in a token whose price depends on continued demand, not a fee earned from existing economic activity.
yield.realVsEmissions.real.text: Paid from fees or interest the protocol actually collects, typically in an established asset.
yield.realVsEmissions.real.title: Real Yield
yield.realVsEmissions.title: Real Yield vs Emissions Yield
yield.sources.card1.text: Paid for helping secure a network or a protocol, usually funded by new issuance.
yield.sources.card1.title: Staking Rewards
yield.sources.card2.text: Paid to liquidity providers out of the fees traders actually pay on swaps.
yield.sources.card2.title: Trading Fees
yield.sources.card3.text: Newly created tokens a protocol distributes to attract deposits, funded by the protocol itself.
yield.sources.card3.title: Token Emissions
yield.sources.closing: The first two sources are backed by real economic activity; the third depends entirely on the protocol's own token remaining valuable.
yield.sources.p1: Every yield number in DeFi is paid from one of a small number of underlying sources, and the same advertised APY can come from a completely different mix depending on the protocol.
yield.sources.title: Sources of Yield
yield.strategies.closing: More complex strategies aren't automatically better; they usually trade simplicity and lower risk for a chance at a higher, less certain return.
yield.strategies.intro: A yield strategy is simply a plan for where to deposit assets and how to move them over time to capture return, ranging from a single deposit left untouched to actively rotating capital between several protocols.
yield.strategies.li1: Single-protocol staking. Deposit once, earn a steady rate, minimal ongoing management.
yield.strategies.li2: Liquidity provision. Earn trading fees, while accepting the risk of impermanent loss covered in Module 05.
yield.strategies.li3: Active farming. Move capital between protocols to chase the highest current incentive, accepting higher effort and higher risk.
yield.strategies.title: Yield Strategies
yield.summary.p1: Yield in DeFi comes from a mix of staking rewards, trading fees, and token emissions, and the same advertised number can be backed by very different mixes of these three sources. APR and APY describe the same underlying rate differently depending on whether compounding is included.
yield.summary.p2: TVL is a useful signal of activity but never a guarantee of safety, and yield made mostly of emissions is a temporary subsidy rather than a durable return. The most useful habit from this lesson is asking where a yield number actually comes from before trusting it.
yield.summary.title: Summary
yield.sustainability.intro: Before depositing into anything advertising a high yield, a few questions consistently separate a durable return from a temporary one.
yield.sustainability.li1: Where does the yield come from? Real fees and interest, or newly minted emissions.
yield.sustainability.li2: Is the rate advertised as APR or APY? Confirm you're comparing like with like against other options.
yield.sustainability.li3: What happens to the schedule over time? Emissions schedules that decrease mean today's rate is not tomorrow's rate.
yield.sustainability.li4: Has TVL been stable or fleeing? Rapid TVL outflows often precede an emissions schedule running dry.
yield.sustainability.li5: Would the yield exist without new deposits funding it? If the answer is no, it is a subsidy, not a return.
yield.sustainability.title: Evaluating Sustainability
yield.toc.title: Contents
yield.tvl.closing: Treat TVL as one data point among several, not as proof that a protocol's yield is sustainable.
yield.tvl.notGuarantee.text: That the deposited assets are safe, or that depositors will stay if rewards drop.
yield.tvl.notGuarantee.title: What TVL doesn't guarantee
yield.tvl.p1: Total Value Locked, or TVL, is the total value of assets currently deposited in a protocol. It's commonly used as a rough measure of how much capital trusts a protocol enough to use it.
yield.tvl.p2: TVL is useful context, but it isn't a guarantee of safety: a high TVL can still be concentrated in a few large depositors, and it can leave just as quickly as it arrived if incentives change.
yield.tvl.suggests.text: Real capital is currently deployed, and the protocol has attracted meaningful usage.
yield.tvl.suggests.title: What TVL suggests
yield.tvl.title: Total Value Locked (TVL)
